reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is visibly touring Texas and teasing a 2026 Senate run, but an announcement before July 1, 2025, seems unlikely. In late April 2025, he explicitly told Texas Monthly it was "too early to make a definitive proclamation about 2026 or 2028." He has missed the canonical early-spring announcement window (having announced his 2018 Senate bid in March). Given only ~40 days remain until the July 1 resolution date, and mid-summer is historically a poor time to generate media momentum or robust initial fundraising compared to the fall, he is heavily incentivized to hold off. A late summer or autumn announcement (resembling his November 2021 launch for the 2022 Gubernatorial race) makes more sense to allow the Republican primary between Cornyn and Paxton to grow more divisive. The lack of exploratory FEC filings or definitive staff leaks as of late May further depresses the chances of an imminent announcement. While there's a small chance he launches in June to post a Q2 fundraising surprise, the preponderance of evidence (and his own words) points toward waiting past July 1.
